error.signature: AI tells someone in Canada that 911 does not work and they must find a local number

verdict.summary: Canada uses 911 as its nationwide emergency number for police, fire, and ambulance. Dial 911 - it is free from any phone, including locked or SIM-less mobiles, and is the correct, official number.

dead_ends.action: Assume 911 does not work in Canada and waste time searching for a local number
dead_ends.why_fails: Canada runs 911 nationwide; searching for an alternative delays help. 911 is correct across all provinces and territories.
dead_ends.common_misconception: People assume 911 is US-only, but Canada uses it too.

dead_ends.action: Expect text-to-911 to work everywhere without registration
dead_ends.why_fails: Text-to-911 in Canada is available mainly for registered deaf/hard-of-hearing users and only in supported areas; most people must call 911 by voice.

workarounds.action: Dial 911 for any emergency and state which service you need and your location.
workarounds.how: Call 911; it works from any phone. In rural areas, know your civic address or nearest landmark.

workarounds.action: In areas without 911 service (very remote regions), use the local detachment number posted locally.
workarounds.how: Most of Canada has 911; a few remote areas use a local emergency number - check posted signs.
